posts.body: Predictions: 5 of 5 Pending | Tied for 1st with 499 others We obviously don't know the actual athletic shape McGregor's in, or how much ring rust he's got, but I imagine he's not ready to fight someone so high level at this stage of his life. That said, I think Conor vs Charles is a better fight both promotion wise (BMF) and it's a better stylistic matchup for McGregor, even though I think he loses badly against both guys. * Edited at 04.28.2026, 1:05 AM ET *

posts.author: Aceofheartss88
posts.body: Predictions: 5 of 5 Pending | Tied for 1st with 499 others @B7L7 how is Charles Oliveira (a grappler and all-time submission wins leader) a better stylistic matchup for Connor McGregor then Max Holloway is? Max Holloway has probably never attempted a take down in his UFC career and is going to stay on the feet with Conor McGregor and box with him. Holloway is no doubt the better boxer but he doesn’t have the power to knock McGregor out, so McGregor has a punchers chance to catch Holloway with something and win the fight, but Holloway probably wins by decision… whereas Charles Oliveira knows McGregor can’t stop the takedown and he can out grapple McGregor in his sleep so he will do the same thing he did with Max…. Pursue the takedown ASAP and look to submit Conor which he probably will because Max is way harder to finish on the ground then Conor is, at least historically… The fact that you posted that tells me you know absolutely nothing about fighting or what a matchup actually is lol also, the UFC probably doesn’t want to put McGregor in a fight where he’s just going to get taken to the ground and submitted for his comeback fight. They want Connor to have a good showing because at the end of the day they want to keep him fighting because he is their biggest draw and has the most earning potential so they give him a fight where they know he’s going to stay on his feet, which is a fight with Max Holloway

posts.author: B7L7
posts.body: Predictions: 5 of 5 Pending | Tied for 1st with 499 others @Aceofheartss88 let me get home from work, I'll answer to this in like 30 minutes.

posts.author: B7L7
posts.body: Predictions: 5 of 5 Pending | Tied for 1st with 499 others @Aceofheartss88 alright, here I am, so:1. I remember watching the first fight between Conor and Max which was 2013 (I hate assuming, but since you assumed I know nothing about fighting, I’m automatically going to assume you were 5 years old in 2013, so deal with it😂😎). I haven’t rewatched it since but remember Conor wrestling a lot. Max is levels above that version of himself, and his volume is overwhelming for most guys. Conor McGregor is about to be 38, and between his age, number of serious training sessions or extended periods of training since 2021 that you can count on one hand, and the ring rust, he’s a shell of a fighter he once was, and isn’t a counter sniper. 2. Puncher’s chance is the most overused and overrated term in MMA. If anything, I’d argue Conor has more of a punchers chance against Charles than against Max. Obviously Max is less durable now, but he’s also less chinny than Charles and has way better boxing defense. Charles is super stationary, has dangerous offensive muay thai, but he’s very susceptible to boxing counters (even though I already said Conor’s not going to be a super quick counter striker). Charles gets tagged on the feet in every fight he has. Another thing, he’s an elite submission grappler, but not an elite wrestler. I don’t know what you’ve been watching but he won’t be able to take down Conor at will. Prime Khabib wasn’t able to do that against coked up Conor, and someone who’s 2 levels below Khabib in pure wrestling shouldn’t be able to do that, even though it will be easier as Conor gets more tired.Now I still think both guys smash Conor in this day and age, I just wanted to give you educated reasoning that you don’t have to agree with, but I hope you can respect other people’s opinions without automatically having to be rude towards them, like that one fool over here who called another guy’s mom a ***** because he bet on a different fight outcome. * Edited at 04.28.2026, 9:49 AM ET *

posts.author: Gilpin
posts.body: @B7L7 I can see what you're getting at, but lets be realistic. A lot of people act like Conor is a celebrity like CM Punk who is naive to think they can fight in the UFC and they straight up give him no chance in a fight with any ranked opponent. We have to remember that he was legitimately the best striker in the UFC at one point and that skill doesn't leave you, I think his biggest flaw in a fight with Max is his endurance and chin, but striking wise McGregor still has the skill to make it competitive. That being said, if the fight were to happen... give me Max by round 3 TKO

posts.author: novaCMMA
posts.body: Predictions: 5 of 5 Pending | Tied for 1st with 499 others @Gilpin Conor was probably the best counter striker we've ever seen, hence why he reached the heights he did. But i think his style is pretty much figured out. I mean he lost against a Nate Diaz (In His Prime mind you) who he was supposed walk through and stop. Fought a prime Khabib and was smoked but no shame there. Fought a Prime Porrier and was TKO'd. (I'm only counting their second fight btw). I think Ferguson decimates him, barboza was a tough out. People scroll on tik tok and see an edit of him knocking out Aldo and tko'ing Marcus Brimage and say with a straight face he is the greatest fw or lw ever. The guy was amazing and had exceptional talent, but any time the fight was taken to HIM, he looked for a way out. Im not saying he wouldnt crack max a few times and hurt him, but I'd be shocked to see conor any higher than +200 * Edited at 04.28.2026, 7:39 PM ET *

posts.author: B7L7
posts.body: Predictions: 5 of 5 Pending | Tied for 1st with 499 others @Gilpin the skill doesn't leave you, but when you're physically washed you can't show what you got. I can use this example on myself since I played pro football/soccer before I started training MMA and was technically a very good player. But, when I stopped training for an extended period of time and I tried to come back at semi pro level, I couldn't showcase my technical skill in a match because I just didn't have the energy and strength to do it consistently. When you're tired and weak your body behaves differently. Now imagine a 38 year old Conor McGregor, who's not a footballer but a CONTACT combat sport athlete, meaning he's absorbed a lot of damage throughout his career. As I already said, since 2021 you can probably count on one hand the number of times he had tough training sessions. I agree the skill doesn't leave you, but you are unable to show it when you don't have the physicals to match it. * Edited at 04.29.2026, 3:57 AM ET *

posts.author: IlikeMMAandboobs
posts.body: Saint-Denis vs. PimblettBenoit Saint-Denis, TKO, R3Benoit has power in his hands and a good grappling: he has a 100% finish rate. Paddy has a chin for sure, but let's remember he got dropped by Gaethje a few times, with the difference that Benoit can engage on the ground too, unlike Gaethje, whose ground-and-pound isn't as good as the Benoit's one. I think Paddy will end up on the ground in the later rounds and get TKO'ed. Could be one of the most amazing fights of the last years.
